title: ptelea trifoliata f. pubescens
intro: Ptelea trifoliata f. pubescens, also known as the rough-leaf horse gentian, is a fragrant, flowering shrub native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The rough-leaf horse gentian has been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, providing relief and promoting overall well-being. Herbal preparations of Ptelea trifoliata f. pubescens can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions, allowing for a convenient and natural way to harness its medicinal benefits.
